items.title: Chrome V8 Zero-Day CVE-2026-11645 Exploited in the Wild — Patch Now
items.summary: Google patched a high-severity out-of-bounds memory access flaw (CVSS 8.8) in Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. Google confirmed an exploit exists in the wild, making this the latest in a string of actively exploited Chrome zero-days in 2026.
items.why: Chrome V8 zero-days are prime targets for drive-by browser exploitation. Any unpatched Chromium-based browser is at risk; update to the latest stable channel immediately.

items.title: Critical Check Point VPN Zero-Day CVE-2026-50751 Under Active Exploitation Since May
items.summary: A critical zero-day vulnerability in Check Point VPN appliances has been under active exploitation since at least May 7, 2026, with attack volume intensifying in early June. Rapid7 published an emergency threat response detailing observed attacker behavior.
items.why: VPN appliances are high-value initial-access targets. Successful exploitation can grant attackers full network entry — organizations should apply the patch and audit VPN logs immediately.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes CVE-2026-21509 in 24 Hours, Hits Eastern European Military via Cloud C2
items.summary: Russian state-sponsored APT28 (Fancy Bear) launched a concentrated 72-hour spear-phishing campaign targeting military and government entities across nine Eastern European nations, weaponizing the Microsoft Office zero-day CVE-2026-21509 within 24 hours of public disclosure and using cloud-hosted C2 infrastructure to evade detection.
items.why: The 24-hour exploit weaponization window and cloud C2 abuse demonstrate how quickly nation-state actors operationalize new vulnerabilities — patch timelines measured in days are no longer sufficient.

items.title: Iran's 'Screening Serpens' APT Escalates Espionage Campaigns Across Five Countries in 2026
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit42 is tracking an Iranian APT group dubbed Screening Serpens that has dramatically increased operations following the regional conflict beginning in February 2026, deploying two new RAT malware families against targets in up to five countries.
items.why: Geopolitical escalation is directly driving Iranian APT tempo. The deployment of new, previously undocumented RAT families signals active tooling investment and expanding targeting scope.

items.title: CrowdStrike 2026 Global Threat Report: AI Slashes Attacker Breakout to 29 Minutes, GenAI Itself Now Targeted
items.summary: CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report reveals that AI-accelerated adversaries reduced average eCrime breakout time to 29 minutes (fastest observed: 27 seconds). Additionally, threat actors injected malicious prompts into enterprise GenAI tools at over 90 organizations, establishing GenAI platforms as a new attack surface.
items.why: A 29-minute breakout window demands near-real-time detection and response capabilities that most organizations lack. The targeting of GenAI systems themselves is a structural new threat requiring dedicated controls.
